title: lamium moschatum subsp. micranthum
intro: Lamium moschatum subsp. micranthum, also known as small-flowered spurge laurel or small-flowered lamium, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Asia Minor. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties due to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Lamium moschatum subsp. micranthum include its potential to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and act as an antibacterial agent. Traditional herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
